calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To Nov-16-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Jessica Jewell'S Compliance With Prior Responses To Defendant'S Request For Production Of Documents [Set One]
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Joyce Wing Yin Leung's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Jessica Jewell's Compliance With Prior Responses To Defendant's Request For Production Of Documents is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.210, which provides: "The party to whom a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling has been directed shall respond separately to each item or category of item by any of the following: (1) A statement that the party will comply with the particular demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling by the date set for the inspection, copying, testing, or sampling pursuant to paragraph (2) of subdivision (c) of Section 2031.030 and any related activities. (2) A representation that the party lacks the ability to comply with the demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling of a particular item or category of item. (3) An objection to the particular demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling."

Defendant moves, as well, under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.320. Section 2031.320(a) provides: "If a party filing a response to a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling under Sections 2031.210, 2031.220, 2031.230, 2031.240, and 2031.280 thereafter fails to permit the inspection, copying, testing, or sampling in accordance with that party's statement of compliance, the demanding party may move for an order compelling compliance."

Defendant contends Plaintiff's production was incomplete.

As to the production in response to the following RFPs, Defendant failed to demonstrate Plaintiff's production was incomplete: RFP, nos. 10, 20, 30, 31, 51,

As to the production in response to the following RFPs, Defendant has demonstrated Plaintiff's production was incomplete and further production is required: RFP, no. 11,

Further production in consistent with response to RFP, no. 11 no later than 15 days after notice of this order.

Plaintiff's "jurisdiction" objection is overruled.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2025 Set For Trial On Jun-08-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
